name: ETH Value Recovery Scale-In v1
edge_mechanism: broad value-recovery mean reversion with staged confirmation
thesis: ETH 1h is the best-fit primary lane for testing scale-in around value recovery when confirmation improves across bars instead of one-shot all-in timing. In a rangy but still transitional intraday tape, repaired value behavior should be denser than pure continuation entries.
invalidation_condition: Abort if value-recovery entries remain too sparse, if the confirmation leg rarely appears, or if staged complexity adds no measurable post-cost benefit over simpler recovery logic.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price stretches away from short-term value, reclaims balance, then confirms recovery on the following bar so size can be staged rather than committed all at once.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In choppy intraday conditions, value repair should recur more often than clean breakouts. Scaling in after a reclaim plus second confirmation may reduce bad first-touch entries without killing density.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 1h offers frequent value-recovery structure with manageable noise and enough liquidity for clean stress-testing.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If price only snaps back to value and stalls, the added scale-in leg becomes unnecessary complexity with no edge gain.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is acceptable but second-leg confirmation adds no value, simplify to one-shot entry while preserving the recovery thesis.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Fairly frequent entries, modest average winners, and improved expectancy only if staged sizing meaningfully reduces bad first-touch entries.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 1h plus at least one confirmation lane show real density and better post-cost recovery behavior than a single-shot entry would imply.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, low-trade mirage, or no measurable benefit from the staged confirmation leg.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: low-to-moderate; stretch, reclaim, and confirmation are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: kept broad enough to avoid ceremonial sparse logic

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for frequent value-recovery structure with manageable noise.
validation_basket.continue_if: Density is real and staged confirmation improves trade quality.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Scale-in adds complexity without better outcomes or density collapses.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Repeated reclaim-and-confirm recoveries with tradable but modest range expansion.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same recovery mechanism survives on a slower, cleaner structure.
validation_basket.continue_if: 4h produces fewer but cleaner recoveries with positive post-cost behavior.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The mechanism disappears completely outside 1h noise.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower signal count but cleaner recovery moves than 1h.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the mechanism is structural on majors rather than ETH-specific.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC shows similar recovery behavior with comparable density.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Only ETH works, suggesting asset-specific behavior rather than general value repair.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Slightly cleaner but less explosive recovery signals than ETH.

trade_management.add_on_condition: Next bar closes in trade direction and RSI_14 improves by at least 2 points while DI handoff remains intact

long: Stop loss: 0.80 * ATR_14 below average entry
short: Stop loss: 0.80 * ATR_14 above average entry
